description_long: This is a dark, turtle-shaped teapot crafted from what appears to be ceramic or clay, featuring a rounded, domed body supported by four short legs. The spout protrudes from the front, cylindrical and slightly tapered, while the top has a wide, open mouth with no visible lid. The surface is adorned with intricate geometric patterns, including zigzags, dots, and cross-hatching, giving it a textured and decorative appearance. The teapot's body is matte and rough, with a hand-crafted, artisanal quality. The handle is not distinctly visible, suggesting it may be integrated into the form or absent. Its compact size and unique design make it suitable as both a functional and decorative object.
description: A dark, turtle-shaped ceramic teapot with geometric patterns, four legs, and a prominent spout. The surface is matte and textured, with a wide open mouth and no visible lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical spout, open mouth, and detailed geometric patterns on the domed body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the three visible legs and the rounded body, with the spout extending from the left.
description_view_2: Top-angled view emphasizing the open mouth, surface patterns, and the four supporting legs.
description_view_3: Rear-side view showing the back of the teapot, the spout on the right, and the decorative motifs wrapping around the body.
attribute_spout_orientation: Straight, cylindrical spout projecting horizontally from the front center of the body.
attribute_handle_design: No distinct handle visible; handle may be absent or integrated into the form.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Geometric patterns including zigzags, dots, and cross-hatching, covering the upper and side surfaces in a single dark color.
